title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Major update
Starting with hypothesis testing, individual .qmd files call the stats_project.py file. The bulk of the code was written when I had very little experience with classes in python and much of this should be restructured. Currently, the code is being adapted to use quarto to render accessible .html documents to meet tile II accessibility guidelines.

title: Practice Problem Generator
section: Documents to produce accessible .html practice problems
text:
Documents to produce accessible .html practice problems
Each document will produces 30 practice problems in a .html slideshow rendered with revealjs. Each slide is scrollable and contains a single problem. Problems are generated with a randomly determined sample size between 5 and 15 per group. When more than one group, sample size is equivalent between groups. * 08 - Hypothesis_Test_z-Scores.qmd: hypothesis testing with z-scores * 09 - Single-Sample_t-Tests.qmd: one-sample t-tests * 10 - Independent-Samples_t-Tests.qmd: independent-samples t-tests * 11 - Dependent-Samples_t-Tests.qmd: dependent-samples t-tests * 12 - one-way_ANOVA.qmd: one-way ANOVA * 13 - repeated-measures_ANOVA: repeated-measures ANOVA * 14 - factorial_ANOVA: two-factor ANOVA

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Future directions

seperate defining question from displaying the results

consider adding: class Question: that will hold the question (this might not be necessary)

will output a formatted assignment and it’s answer key

combine all into a single book style document.
add instructions and a full walkthrough for each problem type

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {894} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {36696} \quad k = {3} \quad N = {24}\) 
\(T_{A} = {359} \quad SS_{A} = {1118.88}\)  \(T_{B} = {307} \quad SS_{B} = {557.88}\)  \(T_{C} = {228} \quad SS_{C} = {630.0}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.74}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {24} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {23}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {24} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {21}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {36696} - \frac{894^2}{24}\]  \[SS_{total} = {36696} - \frac{799236}{24}\]  \[SS_{total} = {36696} - {33301.5}\]  \[SS_{total} = {3394.5}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1118.88 + 557.88 + 630.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {2306.76}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3394.5} - {2306.76}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1087.74}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {8} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {21} - {7}\]  \[df_{error} = {14}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{104^2}{3} + \frac{87^2}{3} + \frac{114^2}{3} + \frac{99^2}{3} + \frac{150^2}{3} + \frac{111^2}{3} + \frac{126^2}{3} + \frac{103^2}{3}} - \frac{894^2}{24}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{10816}{3} + \frac{7569}{3} + \frac{12996}{3} + \frac{9801}{3} + \frac{22500}{3} + \frac{12321}{3} + \frac{15876}{3} + \frac{10609}{3}} - \frac{799236}{24}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{3605.33} + {2523.0} + {4332.0} + {3267.0} + {7500.0} + {4107.0} + {5292.0} + {3536.33}} - {33301.5}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {34162.66} - {33301.5}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {861.16}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {2306.76} - {861.16}\]  \[SS_{error} = {1445.6}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{1087.74}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {543.87}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{1445.6}{14}\]  \[MS_{error} = {103.26}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{543.87}{103.26}\]  \[F_{obt} = {5.27}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{1087.74}{{3394.5} - {861.16}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{1087.74}{2533.34}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.43}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {14}) = {5.27}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.43}\)

\(T_{A} = {479} \quad SS_{A} = {250.92}\)  \(T_{B} = {397} \quad SS_{B} = {110.92}\)  \(T_{C} = {394} \quad SS_{C} = {237.67}\)  \(T_{D} = {487} \quad SS_{D} = {172.92}\)  \(T_{E} = {496} \quad SS_{E} = {336.67}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.78}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {55}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {86565} - \frac{2253^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {86565} - \frac{5076009}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {86565} - {84600.15}\]  \[SS_{total} = {1964.85}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {250.92 + 110.92 + 237.67 + 172.92 + 336.67}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1109.1}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1964.85} - {1109.1}\]  \[SS_{between} = {855.75}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {12} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {11}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {55} - {11}\]  \[df_{error} = {44}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{180^2}{5} + \frac{178^2}{5} + \frac{191^2}{5} + \frac{174^2}{5} + \frac{183^2}{5} + \frac{189^2}{5} + \frac{198^2}{5} + \frac{184^2}{5} + \frac{195^2}{5} + \frac{189^2}{5} + \frac{186^2}{5} + \frac{206^2}{5}} - \frac{2253^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{32400}{5} + \frac{31684}{5} + \frac{36481}{5} + \frac{30276}{5} + \frac{33489}{5} + \frac{35721}{5} + \frac{39204}{5} + \frac{33856}{5} + \frac{38025}{5} + \frac{35721}{5} + \frac{34596}{5} + \frac{42436}{5}} - \frac{5076009}{60}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{6480.0} + {6336.8} + {7296.2} + {6055.2} + {6697.8} + {7144.2} + {7840.8} + {6771.2} + {7605.0} + {7144.2} + {6919.2} + {8487.2}} - {84600.15}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {84777.8} - {84600.15}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {177.65}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {1109.1} - {177.65}\]  \[SS_{error} = {931.45}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{855.75}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {213.94}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{931.45}{44}\]  \[MS_{error} = {21.17}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{213.94}{21.17}\]  \[F_{obt} = {10.11}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{855.75}{{1964.85} - {177.65}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{855.75}{1787.2}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.48}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {44}) = {10.11}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.48}\)

Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {36} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {35}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {36} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {32}\]

Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {9} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {8}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {32} - {8}\]  \[df_{error} = {24}\]

\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{1216.23}{10}\]  \[MS_{error} = {121.62}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{28.22}{121.62}\]  \[F_{obt} = {0.23}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{56.45}{{1344.44} - {71.76}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{56.45}{1272.68}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {10}) = {0.23}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)

\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{615.23}{28}\]  \[MS_{error} = {21.97}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{40.07}{21.97}\]  \[F_{obt} = {1.82}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{80.14}{{764.8} - {69.43}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{80.14}{695.37}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.12}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {28}) = {1.82}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.12}\)

Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {45} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {44}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {45} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {42}\]

Problem 1
Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the population mean: \(\mu = {30}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 30\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 30\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{2.58}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{4}{\sqrt{5}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{4}{2.24}\]  \[\sigma_M = {1.79}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{31.0 - 30}{1.79}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{1.0}{1.79}\]  \[z_{obt} = {0.56}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{31.0 - 30}{4}\]  \[d = \frac{1.0}{4}\]  \[d = {0.25}\]  The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  z = 0.56, p > 0.01, d = 0.25

The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = {-1.645}, \alpha_{one-tailed} = {0.05}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{3}{\sqrt{12}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{3}{3.46}\]  \[\sigma_M = {0.87}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{23.5 - 21}{0.87}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{2.5}{0.87}\]  \[z_{obt} = {2.87}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{23.5 - 21}{3}\]  \[d = \frac{2.5}{3}\]  \[d = {0.83}\]  The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  z = 2.87, p > 0.05, d = 0.83

Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly less than the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({1}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {14.75}, M_B = {16.75}\] \[SS_A = {33.5}, SS_B = {121.5}\] \[n_A = {8}, n_B = {8}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B \geq 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \lt 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = {-2.62}, \alpha_{one-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {14}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{33.5 + 121.5}{7 + 7}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{155.0}{14}\]  \[s_p^2 = {11.07}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{11.07}{8} + \frac{11.07}{8}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.38 + 1.38}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.76}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.66}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(14.75 - 16.75) - {0}}{1.66}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-2.0}{1.66}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-1.2}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(14.75 - 16.75)}{{{\sqrt{11.07}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-2.0)}{3.33}\]  \[d = {-0.6}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(14) = -1.2, p > 0.01, d = -0.6

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {633} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {8549} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {48}\) 
\(T_{A} = {133} \quad SS_{A} = {6.92}\)  \(T_{B} = {141} \quad SS_{B} = {20.25}\)  \(T_{C} = {183} \quad SS_{C} = {10.25}\)  \(T_{D} = {176} \quad SS_{D} = {8.67}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.82}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {48} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {47}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {48} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {44}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {8549} - \frac{633^2}{48}\]  \[SS_{total} = {8549} - \frac{400689}{48}\]  \[SS_{total} = {8549} - {8347.69}\]  \[SS_{total} = {201.31}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {6.92 + 20.25 + 10.25 + 8.67}\]  \[SS_{within} = {46.09}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {201.31} - {46.09}\]  \[SS_{between} = {155.22}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{155.22}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {51.74}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{46.09}{44}\]  \[MS_{within} = {1.05}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{51.74}{1.05}\]  \[F_{obt} = {49.28}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{155.22}{201.31}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.77}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {44}) = {49.28}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.77}\)

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {382} \quad M_{A1B1} = {47.75}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {399.5} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {421} \quad M_{A1B2} = {52.62}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {2403.88} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {439} \quad M_{A1B3} = {54.88}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {864.88} \quad n_{A1B3} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {468} \quad M_{A2B1} = {58.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {1716.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {377} \quad M_{A2B2} = {47.12}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {812.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {314} \quad M_{A2B3} = {39.25}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {339.5} \quad n_{A2B3} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {48} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {47}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {48} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {42}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {128491} - \frac{2401^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {128491} - \frac{5764801}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {128491} - {120100.02}\)  \(SS_{total} = {8390.98}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {399.5 + 2403.88 + 864.88 + 1716.0 + 812.88 + 339.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = {6536.64}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {8390.98} - {6536.64}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1854.34}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1242}  \quad n_{A_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1159}  \quad n_{A_2} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1242}^2}{24} + \frac{{1159}^2}{24}} - \frac{{2401}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1542564}}{24} + \frac{{1343281}}{24}} - \frac{{5764801}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{64273.5} + {55970.04}} - {120100.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {120243.54} - {120100.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {143.52}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{143.52}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {143.52}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{143.52}{155.63}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.92}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{143.52}{{8390.98} - {294.54} - {1416.28}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{143.52}{6680.16}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {42}) = {0.92}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {850}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {798}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {753}  \quad n_{B_3} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.22}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{850}^2}{16} + \frac{{798}^2}{16} + \frac{{753}^2}{16}} - \frac{{2401}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{722500}}{16} + \frac{{636804}}{16} + \frac{{567009}}{16}} - \frac{{5764801}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{45156.25} + {39800.25} + {35438.06}} - {120100.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {120394.56} - {120100.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {294.54}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{294.54}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {147.27}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{147.27}{155.63}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.95}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{294.54}{{8390.98} - {143.52} - {1416.28}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{294.54}{6831.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {0.95}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.22}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1854.34} - {143.52} - {294.54}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1416.28}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1416.28}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {708.14}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{708.14}{155.63}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.55}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1416.28}{{8390.98} - {143.52} - {294.54}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1416.28}{7952.92}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.18}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {4.55}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.18}\)

Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly greater than the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({1}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {34.4}, M_B = {47.2}\] \[SS_A = {121.2}, SS_B = {148.8}\] \[n_A = {5}, n_B = {5}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B \leq 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \gt 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = +{2.9}, \alpha_{one-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {8}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{121.2 + 148.8}{4 + 4}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{270.0}{8}\]  \[s_p^2 = {33.75}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{33.75}{5} + \frac{33.75}{5}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{6.75 + 6.75}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{13.5}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {3.67}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(34.4 - 47.2) - {0}}{3.67}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-12.8}{3.67}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-3.49}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(34.4 - 47.2)}{{{\sqrt{33.75}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-12.8)}{5.81}\]  \[d = {-2.2}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(8) = -3.49, p > 0.01, d = -2.2

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{3.17}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {10}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{37.33 + 38.0}{5 + 5}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{75.33}{10}\]  \[s_p^2 = {7.53}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{7.53}{6} + \frac{7.53}{6}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.26 + 1.26}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.52}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.59}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(20.33 - 16.0) - {0}}{1.59}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{4.33}{1.59}\]  \[t_{obt} = {2.72}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(20.33 - 16.0)}{{{\sqrt{7.53}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(4.33)}{2.74}\]  \[d = {1.58}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(10) = 2.72, p > 0.01, d = 1.58

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.98}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {14}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{600.0 + 702.0}{7 + 7}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{1302.0}{14}\]  \[s_p^2 = {93.0}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{93.0}{8} + \frac{93.0}{8}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{11.62 + 11.62}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{23.24}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {4.82}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(34.5 - 30.5) - {0}}{4.82}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{4.0}{4.82}\]  \[t_{obt} = {0.83}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(34.5 - 30.5)}{{{\sqrt{93.0}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(4.0)}{9.64}\]  \[d = {0.41}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(14) = 0.83, p > 0.01, d = 0.41

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(28.71 - 39.14) - {0}}{1.46}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-10.43}{1.46}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-7.14}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(28.71 - 39.14)}{{{\sqrt{7.52}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-10.43)}{2.74}\]  \[d = {-3.81}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(12) = -7.14, p < 0.01, d = -3.81

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(38.57 - 46.57) - {0}}{2.94}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-8.0}{2.94}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-2.72}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(38.57 - 46.57)}{{{\sqrt{30.28}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-8.0)}{5.5}\]  \[d = {-1.45}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(12) = -2.72, p > 0.01, d = -1.45

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {1158} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {23890} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {60}\) 
\(T_{A} = {233} \quad SS_{A} = {342.92}\)  \(T_{B} = {263} \quad SS_{B} = {48.92}\)  \(T_{C} = {169} \quad SS_{C} = {276.92}\)  \(T_{D} = {236} \quad SS_{D} = {92.67}\)  \(T_{E} = {257} \quad SS_{E} = {314.92}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.68}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {55}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {23890} - \frac{1158^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {23890} - \frac{1340964}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {23890} - {22349.4}\]  \[SS_{total} = {1540.6}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {342.92 + 48.92 + 276.92 + 92.67 + 314.92}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1076.35}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1540.6} - {1076.35}\]  \[SS_{between} = {464.25}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{464.25}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {116.06}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{1076.35}{55}\]  \[MS_{within} = {19.57}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{116.06}{19.57}\]  \[F_{obt} = {5.93}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{464.25}{1540.6}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.3}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {55}) = {5.93}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.3}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {10721} - \frac{569^2}{32}\]  \[SS_{total} = {10721} - \frac{323761}{32}\]  \[SS_{total} = {10721} - {10117.53}\]  \[SS_{total} = {603.47}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {111.88 + 81.88 + 137.5 + 86.88}\]  \[SS_{within} = {418.14}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {603.47} - {418.14}\]  \[SS_{between} = {185.33}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{185.33}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {61.78}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{418.14}{28}\]  \[MS_{within} = {14.93}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{61.78}{14.93}\]  \[F_{obt} = {4.14}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{185.33}{603.47}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.31}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {28}) = {4.14}, p > {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.31}\)

The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {56}) = {13.18}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.41}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{119.22}{17.38}\]  \[F_{obt} = {6.86}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{476.88}{1432.73}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.33}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {55}) = {6.86}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.33}\)

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {55} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {54}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {55} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {50}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7883} - \frac{647^2}{55}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7883} - \frac{418609}{55}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7883} - {7611.07}\]  \[SS_{total} = {271.93}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {26.91 + 40.18 + 28.55 + 28.73 + 54.91}\]  \[SS_{within} = {179.28}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {271.93} - {179.28}\]  \[SS_{between} = {92.65}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{92.65}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {23.16}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{179.28}{50}\]  \[MS_{within} = {3.59}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{23.16}{3.59}\]  \[F_{obt} = {6.45}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{92.65}{271.93}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.34}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {50}) = {6.45}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.34}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {1062} \quad M_{A1B1} = {70.8}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {814.4} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {984} \quad M_{A1B2} = {65.6}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {381.6} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {954} \quad M_{A2B1} = {63.6}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {999.6} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {1047} \quad M_{A2B2} = {69.8}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {1098.4} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {964} \quad M_{A3B1} = {64.27}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {660.93} \quad n_{A3B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {671} \quad M_{A3B2} = {44.73}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {1286.93} \quad n_{A3B2} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {90} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {89}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {90} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {84}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {370706} - \frac{5682^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {370706} - \frac{32285124}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {370706} - {358723.6}\)  \(SS_{total} = {11982.4}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {814.4 + 381.6 + 999.6 + 1098.4 + 660.93 + 1286.93}\)  \(SS_{within} = {5241.86}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {11982.4} - {5241.86}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6740.54}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {2046}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {2001}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1635}  \quad n_{A_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2046}^2}{30} + \frac{{2001}^2}{30} + \frac{{1635}^2}{30}} - \frac{{5682}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{4186116}}{30} + \frac{{4004001}}{30} + \frac{{2673225}}{30}} - \frac{{32285124}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{139537.2} + {133466.7} + {89107.5}} - {358723.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {362111.4} - {358723.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {3387.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{3387.8}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1693.9}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1693.9}{62.4}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {27.15}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3387.8}{{11982.4} - {858.71} - {2494.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3387.8}{8629.66}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.39}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {27.15}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.39}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {2980}  \quad n_{B_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {2702}  \quad n_{B_2} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.95}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2980}^2}{45} + \frac{{2702}^2}{45}} - \frac{{5682}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{8880400}}{45} + \frac{{7300804}}{45}} - \frac{{32285124}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{197342.22} + {162240.09}} - {358723.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {359582.31} - {358723.6}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {858.71}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{858.71}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {858.71}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{858.71}{62.4}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {13.76}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{858.71}{{11982.4} - {3387.8} - {2494.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{858.71}{6100.57}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {84}) = {13.76}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.14}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {6740.54} - {3387.8} - {858.71}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2494.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2494.03}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1247.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1247.02}{62.4}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {19.98}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2494.03}{{11982.4} - {3387.8} - {858.71}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2494.03}{7735.89}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.32}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {19.98}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.32}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 2
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {348} \quad M_{A1B1} = {26.77}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {262.31} \quad n_{A1B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {371} \quad M_{A1B2} = {28.54}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {81.23} \quad n_{A1B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {283} \quad M_{A2B1} = {21.77}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {506.31} \quad n_{A2B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {392} \quad M_{A2B2} = {30.15}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {133.69} \quad n_{A2B2} = {13}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {52} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {51}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {52} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38868} - \frac{1394^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38868} - \frac{1943236}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38868} - {37369.92}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1498.08}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {262.31 + 81.23 + 506.31 + 133.69}\)  \(SS_{within} = {983.54}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1498.08} - {983.54}\)  \(SS_{between} = {514.54}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {719}  \quad n_{A_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {675}  \quad n_{A_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{719}^2}{26} + \frac{{675}^2}{26}} - \frac{{1394}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{516961}}{26} + \frac{{455625}}{26}} - \frac{{1943236}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{19883.12} + {17524.04}} - {37369.92}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {37407.16} - {37369.92}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {37.24}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{37.24}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {37.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{37.24}{20.49}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.82}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {55761} - \frac{1499^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {55761} - \frac{2247001}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {55761} - {53500.02}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2260.98}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {49.71 + 340.86 + 203.43 + 219.71 + 62.86 + 202.86}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1079.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2260.98} - {1079.43}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1181.55}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {755}  \quad n_{A_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {744}  \quad n_{A_2} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{755}^2}{21} + \frac{{744}^2}{21}} - \frac{{1499}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{570025}}{21} + \frac{{553536}}{21}} - \frac{{2247001}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{27144.05} + {26358.86}} - {53500.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {53502.91} - {53500.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {2.89}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{2.89}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {2.89}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{2.89}{29.98}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.1}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.89}{{2260.98} - {394.05} - {784.61}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.89}{1082.32}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {0.1}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {553}  \quad n_{B_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {448}  \quad n_{B_2} = {14}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {498}  \quad n_{B_3} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{553}^2}{14} + \frac{{448}^2}{14} + \frac{{498}^2}{14}} - \frac{{1499}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{305809}}{14} + \frac{{200704}}{14} + \frac{{248004}}{14}} - \frac{{2247001}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{21843.5} + {14336.0} + {17714.57}} - {53500.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {53894.07} - {53500.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {394.05}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{394.05}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {197.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{197.02}{29.98}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {6.57}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{394.05}{{2260.98} - {2.89} - {784.61}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{394.05}{1473.48}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.27}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {6.57}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.27}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1181.55} - {2.89} - {394.05}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {784.61}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{784.61}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {392.3}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{392.3}{29.98}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {13.09}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{784.61}{{2260.98} - {2.89} - {394.05}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{784.61}{1864.04}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.42}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {13.09}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.42}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 6
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {93} \quad M_{A1B1} = {18.6}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {7.2} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {57} \quad M_{A1B2} = {11.4}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {29.2} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {68} \quad M_{A2B1} = {13.6}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {5.2} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {78} \quad M_{A2B2} = {15.6}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {5.2} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {77} \quad M_{A3B1} = {15.4}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {5.2} \quad n_{A3B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {92} \quad M_{A3B2} = {18.4}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {21.2} \quad n_{A3B2} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {30} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {29}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {30} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {24}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7473} - \frac{465^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7473} - \frac{216225}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7473} - {7207.5}\)  \(SS_{total} = {265.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {7.2 + 29.2 + 5.2 + 5.2 + 5.2 + 21.2}\)  \(SS_{within} = {73.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {265.5} - {73.2}\)  \(SS_{between} = {192.3}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {150}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {146}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {169}  \quad n_{A_3} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.61}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{150}^2}{10} + \frac{{146}^2}{10} + \frac{{169}^2}{10}} - \frac{{465}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{22500}}{10} + \frac{{21316}}{10} + \frac{{28561}}{10}} - \frac{{216225}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{2250.0} + {2131.6} + {2856.1}} - {7207.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {7237.7} - {7207.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {30.2}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{30.2}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {15.1}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{15.1}{3.05}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {4.95}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{30.2}{{265.5} - {4.04} - {158.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{30.2}{103.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {4.95}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {238}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {227}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.82}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{238}^2}{15} + \frac{{227}^2}{15}} - \frac{{465}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{56644}}{15} + \frac{{51529}}{15}} - \frac{{216225}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{3776.27} + {3435.27}} - {7207.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {7211.54} - {7207.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {4.04}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{4.04}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {4.04}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{4.04}{3.05}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.32}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4.04}{{265.5} - {30.2} - {158.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{4.04}{77.24}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {1.32}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.05}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.61}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {192.3} - {30.2} - {4.04}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {158.06}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{158.06}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {79.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{79.03}{3.05}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {25.91}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{158.06}{{265.5} - {30.2} - {4.04}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{158.06}{231.26}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.68}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {25.91}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.68}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {36309} - \frac{849^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {36309} - \frac{720801}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {36309} - {36040.05}\)  \(SS_{total} = {268.95}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2.8 + 150.8 + 41.2 + 50.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {244.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {268.95} - {244.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = {24.15}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {427}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {422}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{427}^2}{10} + \frac{{422}^2}{10}} - \frac{{849}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{182329}}{10} + \frac{{178084}}{10}} - \frac{{720801}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{18232.9} + {17808.4}} - {36040.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {36041.3} - {36040.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1.25}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.25}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1.25}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.25}{15.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.08}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.25}{{268.95} - {8.45} - {14.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.25}{246.05}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.08}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {418}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {431}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{418}^2}{10} + \frac{{431}^2}{10}} - \frac{{849}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{174724}}{10} + \frac{{185761}}{10}} - \frac{{720801}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{17472.4} + {18576.1}} - {36040.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {36048.5} - {36040.05}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {8.45}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{8.45}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {8.45}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{8.45}{15.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.55}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{8.45}{{268.95} - {1.25} - {14.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{8.45}{253.25}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.55}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {24.15} - {1.25} - {8.45}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {14.45}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{14.45}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {14.45}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{14.45}{15.3}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.94}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{14.45}{{268.95} - {1.25} - {8.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{14.45}{259.25}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.94}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {28896} - \frac{1230^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {28896} - \frac{1512900}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {28896} - {28016.67}\)  \(SS_{total} = {879.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {68.89 + 154.89 + 55.56 + 88.22 + 27.56 + 94.89}\)  \(SS_{within} = {490.01}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {879.33} - {490.01}\)  \(SS_{between} = {389.32}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{143.16}{10.21}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {14.02}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{286.33}{{879.33} - {62.29} - {40.7}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{286.33}{776.34}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.37}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {14.02}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.37}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {586}  \quad n_{B_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {644}  \quad n_{B_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{586}^2}{27} + \frac{{644}^2}{27}} - \frac{{1230}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{343396}}{27} + \frac{{414736}}{27}} - \frac{{1512900}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{12718.37} + {15360.59}} - {28016.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {28078.96} - {28016.67}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {62.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{62.29}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {62.29}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{62.29}{10.21}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {6.1}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{62.29}{{879.33} - {286.33} - {40.7}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{62.29}{552.3}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {6.1}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.19}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {389.32} - {286.33} - {62.29}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {40.7}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{40.7}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {20.35}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{20.35}{10.21}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.99}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {928.78} - {17.02} - {207.26}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {704.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{704.5}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {352.25}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{352.25}{29.68}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {11.87}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{245.07}{{2224.67} - {360.54} - {25.86}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{245.07}{1838.27}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {1.85}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.13}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {753}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {857}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{753}^2}{15} + \frac{{857}^2}{15}} - \frac{{1610}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{567009}}{15} + \frac{{734449}}{15}} - \frac{{2592100}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{37800.6} + {48963.27}} - {86403.33}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {86763.87} - {86403.33}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {360.54}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{360.54}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {360.54}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{360.54}{66.38}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.43}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{360.54}{{2224.67} - {245.07} - {25.86}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{360.54}{1953.74}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.18}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {5.43}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.18}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.4}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {631.47} - {245.07} - {360.54}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {25.86}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{25.86}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {12.93}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{12.93}{66.38}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.19}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{25.86}{{2224.67} - {245.07} - {360.54}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{25.86}{1619.06}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {0.19}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 11
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {407} \quad M_{A1B1} = {50.88}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {174.88} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {391} \quad M_{A1B2} = {48.88}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {888.88} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {423} \quad M_{A2B1} = {52.88}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {1006.88} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {287} \quad M_{A2B2} = {35.88}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {510.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {32} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {31}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {32} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {28}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {75060} - \frac{1508^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {75060} - \frac{2274064}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {75060} - {71064.5}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3995.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {174.88 + 888.88 + 1006.88 + 510.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2581.52}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3995.5} - {2581.52}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1413.98}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {798}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {710}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{798}^2}{16} + \frac{{710}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1508}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{636804}}{16} + \frac{{504100}}{16}} - \frac{{2274064}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{39800.25} + {31506.25}} - {71064.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {71306.5} - {71064.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {242.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{242.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {242.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{242.0}{92.2}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.62}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{242.0}{{3995.5} - {722.0} - {449.98}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{242.0}{2823.52}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {2.62}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {830}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {678}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{830}^2}{16} + \frac{{678}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1508}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{688900}}{16} + \frac{{459684}}{16}} - \frac{{2274064}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{43056.25} + {28730.25}} - {71064.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {71786.5} - {71064.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {722.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{722.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {722.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{722.0}{92.2}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {7.83}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{722.0}{{3995.5} - {242.0} - {449.98}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{722.0}{3303.52}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.22}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {7.83}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.22}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1413.98} - {242.0} - {722.0}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {449.98}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{449.98}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {449.98}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{449.98}{92.2}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.88}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{449.98}{{3995.5} - {242.0} - {722.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{449.98}{3031.5}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {4.88}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.15}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 12
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {324} \quad M_{A1B1} = {27.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {106.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {278} \quad M_{A1B2} = {23.17}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {149.67} \quad n_{A1B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {375} \quad M_{A2B1} = {31.25}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {164.25} \quad n_{A2B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {246} \quad M_{A2B2} = {20.5}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {97.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {12}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {48} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {47}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {48} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {44}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32467} - \frac{1223^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32467} - \frac{1495729}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {32467} - {31161.02}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1305.98}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {106.0 + 149.67 + 164.25 + 97.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {516.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1305.98} - {516.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = {789.06}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {602}  \quad n_{A_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {621}  \quad n_{A_2} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.25}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{602}^2}{24} + \frac{{621}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1223}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{362404}}{24} + \frac{{385641}}{24}} - \frac{{1495729}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{15100.17} + {16068.38}} - {31161.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {31168.55} - {31161.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {7.53}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{7.53}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {7.53}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{7.53}{11.75}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.64}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{7.53}{{1305.98} - {638.03} - {143.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{7.53}{524.45}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {44}) = {0.64}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {699}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {524}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.25}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{699}^2}{24} + \frac{{524}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1223}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{488601}}{24} + \frac{{274576}}{24}} - \frac{{1495729}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{20358.38} + {11440.67}} - {31161.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {31799.05} - {31161.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {638.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{638.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {638.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{638.03}{11.75}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {54.3}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{638.03}{{1305.98} - {7.53} - {143.5}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{638.03}{1154.95}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.55}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {44}) = {54.3}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.55}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.25}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {789.06} - {7.53} - {638.03}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {143.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{143.5}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {143.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{143.5}{11.75}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {12.21}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{143.5}{{1305.98} - {7.53} - {638.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{143.5}{660.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.22}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {44}) = {12.21}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.22}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 13
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {185} \quad M_{A1B1} = {26.43}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {161.71} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {232} \quad M_{A1B2} = {33.14}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {290.86} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {217} \quad M_{A2B1} = {31.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {428.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {241} \quad M_{A2B2} = {34.43}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {207.71} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {164} \quad M_{A3B1} = {23.43}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {241.71} \quad n_{A3B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {236} \quad M_{A3B2} = {33.71}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {359.43} \quad n_{A3B2} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {42} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {41}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {42} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {36}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {41091} - \frac{1275^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {41091} - \frac{1625625}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {41091} - {38705.36}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2385.64}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {161.71 + 290.86 + 428.0 + 207.71 + 241.71 + 359.43}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1689.42}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2385.64} - {1689.42}\)  \(SS_{between} = {696.22}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {417}  \quad n_{A_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {458}  \quad n_{A_2} = {14}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {400}  \quad n_{A_3} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.25}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{417}^2}{14} + \frac{{458}^2}{14} + \frac{{400}^2}{14}} - \frac{{1275}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{173889}}{14} + \frac{{209764}}{14} + \frac{{160000}}{14}} - \frac{{1625625}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{12420.64} + {14983.14} + {11428.57}} - {38705.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {38832.35} - {38705.36}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {126.99}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{126.99}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {63.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{63.5}{46.93}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.35}\)
